rationale: The citing paper explicitly situates its contribution within country/factory-of-origin research and argues prior COO work focused on quality-based mechanisms, while the cited paper is a core COO study (“Cultural Variations in Country of Origin Effects”) that theorizes when COO cues shape evaluations across cultures. Given this direct construct overlap (COO effects, origin-based evaluation mechanisms), the citation could be foundational/theory-relevant rather than a purely incidental mention, even though the raw match only provides DOI-level evidence.
